steps.title: Use the discriminant
steps.content:
A quadratic has more than one real solution when its discriminant is positive. For $77x^2+bx+c=0$, use $a=77$:
$$
b^2-4(77)c>0
$$
$$
b^2-308c>0
$$

steps.title: Solve for the relationship
steps.content: The inequality becomes: $$ b^2>308c $$ Since $b$ is positive, take the positive square root: $$ b>\sqrt{308c} $$ The answer is B.
